news.headline: Gold prices hit new all-time high at $3,056 per ounce as demand spikes due to economic woes, up 16.21% in 2025.
news.summary: On Wednesday, gold prices reached a record high after the Federal Reserve forecasted a decline in economic growth and increased inflation. Spot gold peaked at $3,056 per ounce before slightly falling back, while gold futures on the Commodity Exchange surged to $3,060.20. This surge in gold prices is driven by a heightened demand for safe-haven assets amidst uncertainties fueled by President Trump's tariff actions. Year-to-date, spot gold has risen by over 16.21%. The Federal Reserve's announcement contributed to the upward trend in gold prices as investors seek stability in their portfolios.

news.headline: Bristol Myers Squibb appoints Osama Braiwish as General Manager for Saudi Arabia & Gulf Countries, overseeing 4 countries.
news.summary: In Riyadh, Saudi Arabia, on March 20, 2025, Bristol Myers Squibb, a prominent biopharmaceutical company, has announced the appointment of Osama Braiwish as the new General Manager for Saudi Arabia and Gulf Countries. Braiwish will oversee operations in Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates, Kuwait, Qatar, and other affiliated nations.

news.headline: Pakistan seeks to attract global investors by establishing a crypto legal framework, aiming to lead in blockchain finance. With 20M crypto users, Chainalysis ranks Pakistan 9th for adoption. CEO claims US President Trump is encouraging crypto adoption. Pakistan plans to leverage blockchain for remittances and trade to reduce fees and enhance global payment networks.
news.summary: Pakistan is aiming to attract international investors by creating a legal framework for crypto. The CEO of Pakistan Crypto Council, Bilal Bin Saqib, stated that the country is looking for regulatory clarity and a pro-business legal framework. Pakistan ranks ninth for crypto adoption, with approximately 20 million crypto users. Saqib believes that Pakistan has a young workforce ready to lead in blockchain-powered finance. He also mentioned the potential benefits for developing nations like Pakistan and Nigeria through blockchain usage, specifically in reducing fees and creating seamless cross-border payment networks. Saqib was appointed as CEO on March 14 and highlighted that Pakistan is open for business in the crypto space. He emphasized the importance of following the footsteps of countries like the US, where even former President Donald Trump has prioritized crypto.

news.headline: Bitcoin likely finds bottom at $77K as analysts say quantitative tightening is effectively over following Fed's announcement to reduce monthly Treasury cap to $5B from $25B in April. Source: CoinMarketCap. BTC up 3.53% in past 7 days. Bullish sentiment rising among investors.
news.summary: The co-founder of BitMEX, Arthur Hayes, believes Bitcoin is unlikely to return to the $77,000 price level soon due to the Federal Reserve's announcement of a slowdown in quantitative tightening. Bitcoin dipped to near $77,000 on March 10 and has since shown a 3.53% increase in the past seven days. Hayes stated that quantitative tightening is essentially over after the Fed's decision to reduce its monthly Treasury cap from $25 billion to $5 billion starting in April. This move could ease liquidity pressures and benefit risk assets like Bitcoin. Jamie Coutts from Real Vision echoed similar sentiments, mentioning that quantitative tightening is effectively dead. The Crypto Fear & Greed Index has moved to neutral territory after being in fear since late February. Despite Bitcoin being down 22% from its all-time high of $109,000 in January, Infinex founder Kain Warwick views this as a normal mid-bull correction, and he remains optimistic about Bitcoin's performance throughout the year based on the four-year cycle theory. Other figures in the crypto industry have also expressed positivity towards the Fed's decision, expecting it to benefit both crypto and equity markets. This article does not provide investment advice, and readers are advised to conduct their own research when making financial decisions.

news.headline: DEMIRE meets 2024 targets for rental income and FFO. Rental income at EUR 65.3m (prev. EUR 78.5m), FFO I at EUR 23.8m (prev. 36.7m).
news.summary: DEMIRE has successfully met its 2024 rental income and FFO guidance with rental income reaching EUR 65.3 million (compared to the previous EUR 78.5 million) and FFO I amounting to EUR 23.8 million (previously at EUR 36.7 million). These figures align with market expectations, showcasing a resilient performance from the company.

Watch out for a dangerous 'cracked' TradingView program laced with malware aiming to steal personal data and drain crypto wallets. Beware of scammers lurking on crypto subreddits offering fraudulent trading programs. Always be cautious of cracked software, which may contain malware. The malicious program has been targeting cryptocurrency wallets and passwords. Malwarebytes discovered the origin of the malware from a Dubai cleaning company website and a Russian registered command and control server. Stay vigilant against AI-driven scams and cyber syndicates in the crypto space, with an estimated $51 billion in illicit transaction volume last year.
news.summary: Malwarebytes has identified a new form of crypto-stealing malware hidden in a cracked version of TradingView Premium, shared on crypto subreddits, targeting crypto wallets and personal data. The malware, containing Lumma Stealer and Atomic Stealer, drains crypto wallets and impersonates victims to further phishing attacks. The scammers offer free, cracked versions of TradingView Premium and assist users in downloading the malware-ridden software. The malware's origin traces back to a Dubai cleaning company’s website and a Russian command and control server. Chainalysis reported $51 billion in illicit transaction volume in 2025, highlighting the surge in professionalized crypto crime. Remember to watch for red flags like disabled security software instructions and password-protected files in such scams.

news.headline: Warning: 'Cracked' TradingView harbors crypto-stealing trojan. Scammers target users with malware-laced installers trapping wallets.
news.summary: Cybersecurity firm Malwarebytes has issued a warning about a new form of crypto-stealing malware found in a "cracked" version of TradingView Premium, used for financial markets charting. Scammers are promoting this malware on crypto subreddits, leading to victims having their crypto wallets emptied and being impersonated. The fraudulent software, claiming to be a free cracked version, actually contains two malicious programs: Lumma Stealer and Atomic Stealer. The scammers assist users in downloading the malware-infected software. Malwarebytes found that the website hosting the files belonged to a Dubai cleaning company, and the malware command and control server was registered in Russia recently. Blockchain analytics firm Chainalysis reported $51 billion in illicit transaction volume in its 2025 Crypto Crime Report, indicating a rise in professionalized AI-driven scams and efficient cyber syndicates. This incident serves as a reminder to watch out for red flags in software downloads to avoid falling victim to malware attacks.

news.headline: Economist Mohamed El-Erian criticizes Fed for calling inflation 'transitory' amid tariff war. Fed holds rates steady at 4.25%-4.5%, projects one rate cut (previously two), reduces quantitative tightening from $25 billion to $5 billion starting April. El-Erian expresses disappointment in Fed's language.
news.summary: Senior economist Mohamed El-Erian criticized Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell for using the word “transitory” to describe potential inflationary impact of tariffs. The Federal Reserve kept rates steady at 4.25%-4.5%, with a median forecast of two rate cuts for the year, though a shift toward one cut was hinted in ‘dot plot’ projections. Powell mentioned ‘transitory’ inflation and announced a decrease in balance sheet reduction from $25 billion to $5 billion in April. El-Erian expressed disappointment in the Fed's language, citing past inaccuracies in using 'transitory' to describe inflation in the early 2020s.

news.headline: VEON reports strong performance in FY24 and 4Q24, fueled by digital services growth.
Investor urges vote against BP chairman Lund over climate concerns.
news.summary: A new report reveals that the global population has surpassed 7.8 billion people, with an increase of 82 million individuals compared to the previous year. The data shows that Asia remains the most populous continent, accounting for approximately 60% of the total population. In contrast, Africa is identified as the fastest-growing region, with a growth rate of 2.7% per year. These findings underscore the continued expansion of the world's population and highlight the shifting demographics across different continents.

news.headline: Galderma at AMWC 2025 in Monaco with 2 symposia, 4 Masterclass sessions, 11 e-posters, 5 Meet-the-Expert sessions.
news.summary: ZUG, Switzerland - Leading pharmaceutical company Galderma (SWX: GALD) is set to have a significant presence at the 23rd Aesthetic & Anti-Aging Medicine World Congress (AMWC) in Monaco from March 27-29, 2025. The company will host two symposia, conduct four Masterclass sessions, present 11 research e-posters, and offer an experiential Galderma lounge at booth O2 in Hall Ravel. Alongside, there will be five Meet-the-Expert sessions organized at their interactive booth. This comprehensive program showcases Galderma's cutting-edge Injectable Aesthetics portfolio, demonstrating their expertise in the field.

news.headline: Valneva SE reveals full year 2024 financial results & future outlook. Check website for detailed report.
news.summary: On March 20, 2025, Valneva SE, a specialty vaccine company, announced its financial results for the year ended December 31, 2024. The company, listed on Nasdaq under VALN and Euronext Paris under VLA, shared key corporate updates and future guidance along with the release of its consolidated financial results. Interested parties can access the detailed financial reports on Valneva's website under the "Financial Reports" section.

news.headline: Sodexo updates first half of fiscal 2025 estimates and full-year guidance.
news.summary: A total of 735 new cases have been reported, bringing the overall number of confirmed COVID-19 infections in the country to 25,678. Additionally, four more deaths were recorded, raising the death toll to 578. The number of active cases currently stands at 9,324, with 15,776 individuals having recovered from the virus. The government continues to emphasize the importance of practicing preventive measures such as wearing masks, practicing social distancing, and frequent handwashing to curb the spread of the virus.

Trump Media executives raise $179M via SPAC for potential acquisition of US-based crypto or blockchain firm, amid sector support.
news.summary: Three executives at Trump Media & Technology Group are spearheading an effort to acquire a US-based crypto or blockchain firm through a special-purpose acquisition company (SPAC) called Renatus Tactical Acquisition Corp I. The SPAC aims to raise over $178.94 million through 17.5 million public shares at $10 each and 3.94 million private placement warrants at $1 each. Renatus Tactical's focus is on businesses related to data security, military and non-military technology, and potentially industries worldwide, with a preference for US-based high-potential firms. The company highlighted Trump administration's support for digital assets, citing executive orders on Bitcoin reserves and crypto laws. However, concerns exist over potential hesitancy to engage with Renatus Tactical due to ties with Trump and Trump Media. Trump holds a majority stake in Trump Media, with Forbes estimating his wealth at $4.8 billion and Bloomberg at over $6.5 billion, with $2.36 billion attributed to his shares in Trump Media. Trump put these shares into a trust to address conflict of interest concerns. There are comparisons made between Renatus Tactical's potential issues with Trump ties and Tesla's share price decline due to Elon Musk's role as White House cost-cutting czar. Forbes raises questions about conflict of interest and insider trading regarding Trump's crypto ventures.

news.headline: Bitcoin's price dips during economic crises due to increased market uncertainty, temporary decrease in value explained by Bitwise CIO.
news.summary: Bitwise's CIO, Matt Hougan, discussed Bitcoin's behavior during market crises, noting its contradictory nature despite being seen as an inflation hedge. Hougan attributed short-term price drops during economic turmoil to heightened market uncertainty, which increases the perceived risk of the asset, resulting in a temporary decrease in value, known as a "discount factor." Hougan cited tariff concerns as an example, which have impacted Bitcoin recently. While tariffs raise short-term risk, they can also increase the long-term price target. For more details, refer to the full story on Benzinga.com.

Helsinki, Finland ranks as happiest country for 8th year w/ avg score of 7.7/10 - core of happiness remains constant.
news.summary: Helsinki, as the global leader in happiness, has maintained its top ranking for the eighth consecutive year with an impressive average score of 7.7 out of 10. The city's success highlights its unwavering commitment to cultivating happiness and adapting to new challenges.

Australia cracks down on crypto scammers posing as Binance in Operation Firestorm, disrupting a major scam.
news.summary: Authorities in Australia successfully disrupted a sophisticated crypto scam through Operation Firestorm. The scam involved impersonating Binance, one of the largest cryptocurrency exchanges in the world. This operation resulted in preventing a significant number of individuals from falling victim to the scam and losing their money.

news.headline: Founder of Gotbit surrenders $23M in plea deal tied to crypto market manipulation, may avoid prison and extra fines with court approval.
news.summary: The founder of Gotbit could potentially avoid prison time and additional fines if their plea agreement is approved by the court. This agreement may only require forfeiture without any further financial penalties or jail term.

news.headline: The central bank is uncertain, which is acceptable.
Bitnomial withdraws SEC lawsuit prior to XRP futures launch in the US. CFTC-regulated XRP futures available from March 20 for current users. Regulatory clarity cited.
news.summary: Bitnomial, a Chicago-based crypto exchange, has dropped its lawsuit against the SEC in preparation for launching Ripple XRP futures in the US. The futures, regulated by the CFTC, will be available for users starting March 20. Bitnomial had filed for XRP futures in August 2024 but was blocked by the SEC, leading to the lawsuit. The SEC's jurisdiction over XRP as a security has been disputed, with a judge ruling it not a security for retail sales but possibly for institutional sales. The SEC's ongoing legal battle with Ripple Labs began in December 2020. Amidst changing leadership, the SEC under Mark Uyeda is easing its crypto regulations, including dropping proposed rules on crypto custody standards and potentially including crypto firms as exchanges.

news.headline: Youth at COP29 showcasing creative sustainability solutions at Tuya Smart's booth in NY: translating aesthetics for a sustainable future.
news.summary: In New York on March 20, 2025, during COP29, the climate crisis highlighted not only ecological issues, but also the innovative solutions being developed by a new generation. Tuya Smart showcased these solutions at their booth, where two young individuals demonstrated how they are integrating sustainability into their work.

news.headline: 111, Inc. reports positive financial results with RMB332.7 million YoY improvement in operating profit and decreased operating expenses by 230 and 470 basis points in Q4'24.
news.summary: In the year 2024, the company attained its first-ever annual operating profit, with an increase of RMB332.7 million compared to the previous year. The operating expenses as a percentage of revenues decreased by 230 basis points year over year in 2024. Additionally, in the fourth quarter of 2024, this percentage saw a decrease of 470 basis points compared to the previous year. This significant improvement in financial performance highlights the company's successful cost management strategies and overall growth trajectory.

news.headline: German Avila expected to be next Colombia finance minister
Petro to appoint Avila as finance minister: source
BlackRock's Mitchnick optimistic on Bitcoin in recession, says it thrives on fiscal spending and stimulus. Bitcoin at $86,000 up 3.8%. Meet lawyer Max Burwick.
news.summary: Robbie Mitchnick, head of digital assets at BlackRock, believes that Bitcoin will thrive in a recessionary environment due to factors like increased fiscal spending, deficit accumulation, lower interest rates, and monetary stimulus. Mitchnick stated that Bitcoin tends to be catalyzed by fears of social disorder, which can arise during recessions. He emphasized that Bitcoin should be viewed as uncorrelated or inversely correlated against certain risk factors. Mitchnick mentioned that while some perceive Bitcoin as a risk-on asset, it is still a relatively nascent asset class requiring education. BlackRock has been assisting clients in navigating these conflicting narratives, with some seeing market corrections as buying opportunities. On the other hand, researchers from Coinbase Institutional expressed concerns about crypto markets due to recession fears and recent tariffs. BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF, holding $48.7 billion in net assets, has played a significant role in institutional adoption of Bitcoin. Mitchnick downplayed recent net outflows from Bitcoin exchange-traded funds, attributing them to hedge funds unwinding spot futures arbitrage trades. Bitcoin is currently valued at $86,000, reflecting a 3.8% increase over the last 24 hours.
